title: Smoke
content:
Lord, I have laid my heart upon thy altar
        But cannot get the wood to burn;
    It hardly flares ere it begins to falter
        And to the dark return.

    Old sap, or night-fallen dew, makes damp the fuel;
        In vain my breath would flame provoke;
    Yet see--at every poor attempt's renewal
        To thee ascends the smoke!

    'Tis all I have--smoke, failure, foiled endeavour,
        Coldness and doubt and palsied lack:
    Such as I have I send thee!--perfect Giver,
        Send thou thy lightning back.
meaning:
This evocative piece by George MacDonald, titled "Smoke", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
